## [39:00 - 42:01]

iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi i iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi iwi i Pro Tip No. 2 State a deadline for the client to provide all of the requirements. And if the client does not provide all of the above items within 30 days, this agreement will be cancelled and all fees will be due immediately. So I showed you this in here, that's this piece here. Section 2D, I highly recommend having a deadline in here for the client to provide what we need from them. It just stops the deal from going weird, which can sometimes happen. It's not often, but we want to have something in here to protect us from that, because it's no fun when that happens. So with your consulting agreement and client requirements in place, you are now operating like a pro. And from here, there's only one thing left to do. And that's learning to say no and hold your ground. So contrary to most people's belief, as a consultant, you need to get really good at saying no. Here's why. So learning to say no as a consultant. So consultants get bombarded with requests from their existing clients and potential clients all the time. When you're getting started, it's important to listen to these requests and determine what isn't, isn't necessary in order to achieve the outcome that you're promising. And you need to integrate requests that are necessary and decline everything that isn't necessary. And that's why you need to have a good filter in your mind. That's why you want to try and use first principles instead of reasoning by analogy, because you have to get a good filter. What do I need to add and what do I not need to add? What do I say yes to? What do I say no to? But you're honestly going to be saying no pretty much all the time. You're going to get really good at saying no.

## [42:03 - 45:07]

So once you get to a point where you're in flow and delivering your clients amazing results, it's important that you deflect all additional requests to change the way you work unless it makes absolute sense. And you need to safeguard your magic recipe and your time so that you can help your clients. And this requires saying no. So in the early stages, you're going to have to listen to feedback and you are going to have to integrate some things into your offer. In the beginning, you just can't be like no, no, no, no, no to everyone because quite a lot of the time, I mean you don't even know what you're doing yourself because you're just getting started and you're learning. You're trying new things, some things work, some things don't work. It's an iterative process, right? So here you can say yes to a lot more things but you still want to be conscious that you should also say no. But then once you get into a point where you're delivering results for people and you're in flow, that's when you want to start to say no all the time because you just think about it like this. If we've got a process that's working and it's getting everyone results, why would we change it? Like why would a client want us to mess with variables in a process that are working when it has a higher chance of breaking it than it does improving it? So this is when you want to start getting good at saying no. And when existing clients request additional things from you, you need to be strong and politely inform them that you do not offer what they're asking for. And people never get angry when you say no. But they always try it on when it comes to requesting more from you. So this is an important lesson. Everyone's going to try it on. I mean that's just how people work. They're like he's probably going to say no anyway but it might as well ask, right? So everyone's going to try it on and that's why you need to be good at saying no but don't think that they're going to get angry. They knew you were probably going to say no anyway. So it's fine and you must learn to hold your ground and say no. And one helpful way to say no to your clients is to build up some trusted referral partners who provide stunning results in their respective services. And then refer your clients to them when they ask for things you don't provide. And this way you look more professional, you help your client and you deflect the request and build a referral partner for the future. So for example if someone comes to me and they say, well this happened to me all the time when I was doing consulting back in New Zealand, if someone came to me and they're like I need help with SEO, I would say, well I'm going to do it. If someone came to me and they're like I need help with SEO, I would say, hey, I understand that. I know a little bit about it but I'm not very good at it. And so I don't think you want me doing it. However, I do know one guy who's really good and I'm happy to make a referral to you.

## [45:08 - 48:08]

I'm happy to refer him to you if you want. And that would always be like, oh yeah that'd be amazing, can you do that? So I'd make the referral and then they got the SEO services. They were satisfied and everything. And they were happy and they liked me because I referred them to the right person. And then I built up a friend and a connection that when he got clients that came to him that wanted like ad words or Facebook ads or whatever, he would send them my way. And so you start to build up referral partners. And so things come back to you. If you start helping someone out by sending them some referrals, well they're going to help you out by sending you some referrals. That's just how it works. So that's a really powerful way to say no and help your client and build a referral partner all at the same time. It's kind of like hitting three birds with one stone. So it will feel uncomfortable and wrong at first, but it is the right thing to do. And you can't be a yes man and expect to achieve anything great in your life. By a yes man, I mean someone that just says yes to everything all the time. Oh yeah, I can do that. Yep, yep, we'll do that. No problem. You can't get good at anything if you do that. And in the modern day, it's more about what you ignore rather than what you pay attention to. So a lot of people they think, well, I just need to learn more. I need to follow more things. I need to read more. I need to be on more people's lists. I need to read more newspapers, more magazines, more apps on my phone. I need to consume more. But that is a recipe for failure. In the modern day, it's more about what you ignore. You should be trying to add more things onto your ignore list and you should have hardly anything on your pay attention to list. And here's a awesome quote from Steve Jobs. People think focus means saying yes to the thing you've got the focus on. But that's not what it means at all. It means saying no to the hundred other good ideas that there are. So this is from Steve Jobs. There's a quote from him and this is a really awesome lesson with focus. You know, people are going to ask you to do all sorts of things. Some of the ideas are going to be exciting. People are going to offer to pay you money to do things. And it probably sounds insane to a lot of people listening to this. You mean we say no to money? Yes, when you start making some money. If you're in survival mode, no. Then you don't say no to money. You take the money and you do whatever the work is because you're in survival mode. However, once you do start making some money and you're out of survival mode, say no all the time. Turn down money. I turned down so much money, it's ridiculous. But that's the only way I get to focus on something. And the focus on one thing and on doing what you're supposed to do, it will always end up serving you the best.

## [48:08 - 48:56]

It helps your clients and it helps their businesses. So they're going to pay you more money. You're going to get referrals because your clients are happy. So in the long run, you're going to make more money when you say no. In the short run, you make more money when you say yes. But when you say yes in the long run, you lose. So you get what I'm saying, right? If you say yes, you make more short term, but you lose long term. When you say no, you make less short term, but you make more long term. And we always want to play the long game, provided we're not in survival mode. So that's why this is a powerful quote that you want to remember. You've got to get really good at saying no as a consultant. So that's it for this training module. Thanks for watching and I'll see you in the next one right now.
